growth_picks.name: Applovin Corporation

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 113.1% vs revenue_growth 59% — strong operating leverage.

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest_pct 4.39% is under 5% but short_interest_change_pct +14.79% (rising); meets only one of the two pass conditions.

ai_exposure.detail: Axon AI engine is core to ad-matching and directly underpins the 78% operating margin. Counter-risk: META/GOOG are building competing AI ad engines that could erode the moat.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals present in the input data; cannot assess derivatives positioning or capital intensity.

catalysts_general: Trading 37.1% below the 52-week high of $745.61 — meaningful pullback from highs.
Forward P/E of 21.4x against 59% revenue growth implies PEG of 1.29 — growth-adjusted valuation is reasonable.
Analyst mean target $648.10 implies ~38% upside from $469.39; distribution heavily bullish (28 buy/strong-buy vs 4 hold, 0 sell).
debt_to_equity 162.9% and total_debt $3.85B vs cash $2.76B — leveraged balance sheet is the key risk.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Multiple compression toward sector average if AI-ad competitive concerns (META/GOOG) mount.
Severe de-rating toward the 52-week low ($325.58) if growth decelerates below 40%.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Forward P/E ~19x on $21.93 forward EPS, sitting in the pullback zone between the 52w low and current price; preserves margin of safety while respecting the 59% growth trajectory.

comparables.note: The Trade Desk, closest programmatic-ad peer; NOT in input data, numbers not cited per rules.
Largest AI-powered ad-platform competitor; NOT in input data.

growth_picks.analyst_take: AppLovin posts elite growth-quality (59% revenue / 113% earnings / 88% gross margins / 137 Rule-of-40) at a 21x forward multiple after a 37% pullback — the fundamentals warrant BUY. Key risks are the leveraged balance sheet (D/E 163%), beta 2.45 volatility, and rising short interest, so size for the entry zone rather than chasing.

growth_picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 169.9% vs revenue_growth 55.5% — strong operating leverage.

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest_pct 1.1% is low but short_interest_change_pct +24.59% (rising); only one condition met for pass.

ai_exposure.detail: AI may accelerate Lilly's drug discovery, but the value driver is GLP-1 (Mounjaro/Zepbound) commercialization and manufacturing scale — largely AI-insensitive.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in the input data.

catalysts_general: Only 6.8% below the 52-week high — near highs with strong momentum (+17.3% over 50d, +21.0% over 90d).
Forward P/E 24.8x with PEG 1.47 for a mega-cap pharma — premium but supported by 55.5% growth.
Analyst mean target $1218.72 implies ~11% upside; 24 of 31 analysts are buy/strong-buy.
Dividend yield 0.63%, payout ratio 22.1% — secure and growing; beta 0.52 is defensive.

downside_scenarios.scenario: GLP-1 competitive pressure (Novo orals/generics) compresses the premium multiple.
Regulatory or pipeline setback returns shares to the 52-week low.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Forward P/E ~22.5x on $44.46 forward EPS, near the 50-day SMA ($1014) — a pullback to the moving average offers a lower-risk entry for a high-quality compounder without waiting for a deep drawdown that the 0.52 beta rarely delivers.

comparables.note: Novo Nordisk, direct GLP-1 competitor; NOT in input data.
Large-cap pharma peer; NOT in input data.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Lilly is a best-in-class compounder — 55.5% revenue growth, 170% earnings growth, 83% gross margins, and a 0.52 beta at a 24.8x forward multiple. BUY on quality, though the small 7% discount from highs and only ~11% analyst upside mean new positions are best accumulated toward the $1000 SMA-50 zone rather than chased here.

growth_picks.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 325% vs revenue_growth 84.7% — operating leverage present.

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest_pct 3.23% is under 5% but change +16.82% (rising); only one pass condition met.

Forward P/E 57.4x and P/S 54.8x remain very rich even after the pullback; PEG 1.84 is the highest in the group.

growth_picks.name: Keysight Technologies Inc.

growth_picks.name: Micron Technology, Inc.

earnings_accel.detail: earnings_growth 756% vs revenue_growth 196.3% — operating leverage present.

short_dynamics.detail: short_interest_pct 3.34% (<5%) but change +0.74% (slightly rising); only one pass condition met.

ai_exposure.detail: HBM3E/HBM4 high-bandwidth memory is a critical AI-accelerator component; Micron is a direct beneficiary of the GPU/AI data-center buildout.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in the input data.

catalysts_general: At the 52-week high ($1213.56) after +61.3% in 20 days and +199.6% in 90 days — parabolic, overbought tape.
Forward P/E 10.0x with PEG 0.36 — deeply cheap on forward earnings, reflecting expected HBM-driven earnings explosion (forward EPS $120.73 vs trailing $21.20).
Analyst mean target $945.60 is BELOW the current $1211.38 — analysts are lagging the move, implying ~22% downside to consensus if estimates do not rise.
Trailing P/E 57.1x shows how much depends on the forward earnings materializing; beta 2.17 adds volatility.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Memory-cycle peak: HBM pricing softens as AI-build digestion sets in.
Cyclical earnings cut if 2026 memory oversupply emerges and forward estimates fall.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Forward P/E ~7.5x on $120.73 forward EPS, post-parabolic pullback toward the 20-day SMA zone ($988) and below; captures the AI-HBM earnings thesis without buying at the 52-week high after a 200% run.

comparables.note: NVIDIA, AI-accelerator downstream pull-through; NOT in input data.
AMD, semiconductor AI peer; NOT in input data.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron's forward fundamentals are outstanding — 196% revenue growth, 39.8% ROE, and a 10x forward P/E with PEG 0.36 driven by the HBM/AI cycle — but the stock is at its 52-week high after a +200% 90-day melt-up and the analyst mean target ($945.60) sits well below the current price. WATCHLIST — the thesis is strong, but the parabolic tape and below-market analyst consensus call for a pullback toward ~$900 before initiating.
